assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Ecosystem Detection section explicitly states: "Ecosystem: RPM (not Cargo)" and explains: "openssl-libs is a system-level RPM package, not a source dependency (Cargo/npm/Go). The ecosystem is determined by the package type: openssl-libs is an RPM distributed via Red Hat repositories, present in rpms.lock.yaml. The Ecosystem Mappings table in the 2.2.x stream's security-matrix.md confirms RPM ecosystem with lock file rpms.lock.yaml."

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the version impact table lists all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, openssl-libs 3.0.7-25.el9_3, YES), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, 3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO). The data source is explicitly stated as: "Data source: rpms.lock.yaml at each pinned tag in the 2.2.x supportability matrix."

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Dependency Chain Context section states: "rpms.lock.yaml: present -> explicit install" and "Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs is listed in rpms.lock.yaml)". The explanatory text further confirms: "openssl-libs is classified as an explicit install because it is present in rpms.lock.yaml. It is not inherited solely from the base image -- it is explicitly pinned in the RPM lock file."

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, the heading states "Task: Single System Package Remediation Task". The file explicitly explains: "A single remediation task is created because openssl-libs is an RPM system package ecosystem (not a source dependency). RPM packages do not require an upstream backport task -- the fix happens directly in the Konflux release repo." The section "Why a Single Task (Not Two)" further confirms: "RPM is a system package ecosystem. The remediation happens directly in the Konflux release repo (rhtpa-release.0.4.z)... unlike Cargo/npm/Go source dependencies which require a two-task flow (upstream backport + downstream propagation), RPM system packages need only one task targeting the Konflux release repo." The task targets repository "rhtpa-release.0.4.z" which is the Konflux release repo.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, the task description contains all required sections: "## Repository" (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), "## Target Branch" (main), "## Description" (remediation details with affected/fixed versions), "## Implementation Notes" (update openssl-libs in rpms.in.yaml, regenerate rpms.lock.yaml, verify Konflux build), "## Acceptance Criteria" (two checklist items), "## Test Requirements", and "## Dependencies". There is no "## Files to Modify" section. The output explicitly notes: "Files to Modify section is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md. These depend on repository structure that the triage skill does not have context for -- /implement-task discovers them via code analysis."

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context includes an SBOM verification line — either showing the cosign comparison result or noting that SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Dependency Chain Context block includes the line: "SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available in eval environment". This satisfies the assertion's alternative of noting that SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available.
